topic: China SG null — confirmation by opposite outcomes (post-EXP-209 rewrite)
change_summary: detail_evidence grew 3,347→4,366 chars (+30.4%) since c3 (2026-04-21) via decider self-applies for ISS-1336 (SG noise-floor citation), ISS-1337 (Sun 2010 attribution qualifier), ISS-1338 (PRED-TIER-2/-3 cross-WIN reconciliation note), ISS-1339 (1000x sensitivity rephrased to generation-level spec), and ISS-1349 ('physically meaningless' → precise coupling argument).
our_argument_summary: WIN-014's core argument has been substantially strengthened since c3. The decider closed five issues raised in c3 (ISS-1336/1337/1338/1339/1349). Specifically: (a) Sun 2010 attribution is now hedged with 'attributed 2010; closest verified English-language record: doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 (2013)'; (b) the 1,000x sensitivity claim is now precise (SG noise floors 0.1–1 µGal at minute timescales vs LaCoste-Romberg spring gravimeter); (c) Van Camp 2005 / Crossley 2013 citations now anchor the noise-floor claim; (d) the 'physically meaningless' phrasing is rephrased; (e) a new ISS-1338 cross-reference paragraph reconciles PRED-TIER-2/PRED-TIER-3 across WIN-012 and WIN-014. However, three c3 holes remain unresolved (H1 SEC-8b cross-reference, H5 verdict-text-vs-evidence misalignment, H8 'Eight gravimeters' provenance), and the rewrite introduces two new vulnerabilities: a Level-3-residuals escape-clause the noise-floor citation only partly closes, and a sign-convention drift with WIN-012 (PRED-TIER-2 quoted as '−10 to −13 µGal' here vs predictions.json claim text '10–13 µGal').

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-014 has no monitor.py entry. The dome's 02-wins.txt presents 'Eclipse-Gravity Validation: Beijing 2009' as a static text claim with the value '0.0 µGal' attributed to 'Sun et al., 2010'. No automated check.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static text only; no comparison logic exists in monitor.py for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: PRED-TIER-3 specifies '10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals' — the dome can argue any historical SG null fails to refute the prediction because Level-3 residual-processing wasn't applied. The current WIN-014 text addresses this by citing raw-noise-floor < 1 µGal, but only partially (see H10).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None against monitor.py. The relevant accuracy question is now against predictions.json sign convention and against SEC-8b's continued treatment of 'E-PRED-C' as a published prediction.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/02-wins.txt entry has no domain, no API hook, no python check in monitor.py. Tag 'none' confirmed.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics expects null SG signal during eclipses (no recognized gravitational-shielding mechanism). The dome's claim that the null *confirms* its model is a post-hoc reinterpretation of the default expectation; it is not a renamed-standard-mechanism claim. Tag 'false' defensible. (Same borderline as c3 — keeping false.)
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Eclipse occurred 2009-07-22, dome model is much later. Whatever Sun et al. published (or didn't), the dome adopted the result rather than predicting it in advance.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 0.0 µGal value is observation-adoption; no dome-geometry derivation exists for it.

holes_found.description: C3 H1 still open: WIN-014 detail_evidence asserts 'The dome's current live predictions page (predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) contains no prediction labeled E-PRED-C.' SEC-8b.3 (data/sections.json:part8b.html) still says 'The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor) for the August 2026 eclipse.' Both pages cross-reference each other; one or the other must move. Decider closed ISS-1326 (WIN-014 fabricated E-PRED-C) but did NOT propagate the correction to SEC-8b.3, which still treats E-PRED-A/B/C/D as live dome commitments.
holes_found.recommendation: EXP to reconcile: either (a) update SEC-8b.3 L3a to drop or re-frame the four E-PRED sub-tests as having been removed from the live page (with a date-stamped note), or (b) confirm via a fresh WebFetch that E-PRED-A/B/C/D are or aren't currently published. The 2026-03-12 raw-text snapshot DOES contain all four E-PREDs (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:37-40); the WIN-014 WebFetch from 2026-04-21 said E-PRED-C is absent. Without a 2026-05 verification it is unclear which side is correct. Option (a) is the safer rewrite: cite the 2026-03-12 snapshot as authoritative for SEC-8b's enumeration but add a parenthetical 'as of 2026-03-12 raw-text snapshot; presence on the current live page should be re-verified.'

holes_found.description: C3 H5 still open: detail_verdict_text is stale relative to detail_evidence. Verdict_text reads 'Same unfalsifiable structure as WIN-013: the model claims credit for opposite outcomes across different experiments without advance specification of expected results.' (166 chars, unchanged from c3.) But the new detail_evidence emphasizes a fundamentally different and stronger argument: the dome's PRED-TIER-3 commits to a 10–13 µGal SG pulse at C1/C4 in Aug 2026 — a positive prediction in the same instrument class as the 2009 nulls — making the structure self-contradiction, not merely unfalsifiability. The summary table 'finding' field is also stale (37 chars, 'Same logical contradiction as WIN-013'). The verdict ('Misleading', 10 chars) does not reflect the strengthened self-contradiction argument. WIN-012 has the analogous structure (PRED-TIER-2 vs Mohe historical signal) and is verdicted 'Self-Contradicted' — WIN-014 is its mirror image but stuck at 'Misleading'.
holes_found.recommendation: Verdict change to 'Self-Contradicted' (matching WIN-012) is the c3-recommended action and the c4-confirmed path. Concurrent updates: (a) detail_verdict_text rewritten to lead with PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction (the 'Whichever horn holds, the other fails' framing already in detail_evidence); (b) summary table 'finding' updated to one-line self-contradiction wording; (c) tldr_verdict updated; (d) cross-link to WIN-012's matching self-contradiction. This is the c3 recommended_action='verdict_change' that the decider triaged but did not execute.

holes_found.description: The Level-3-residuals escape-clause is only partly closed by the new noise-floor citation. Current text: 'Superconducting gravimeters of this generation have raw noise floors below 1 µGal at minute timescales (Van Camp, 2005; Crossley et al., 2013), so a 10–13 µGal pulse would appear even without Level-3 processing. It saw none.' This conflates *short-period noise* with *raw signal*. Raw SG signal is dominated by solid-Earth tides (~150–300 µGal peak-to-peak) and atmospheric loading (~10 µGal/mbar); the sub-µGal noise floor is achieved AFTER tide and pressure correction. A 10–13 µGal residual pulse riding on top of an ongoing tide curve might be visible at C1/C4 if the tide model is good and the analyst is looking, but a defender can argue: 'Level-3 residuals' is the dome's explicit specification, and a campaign that didn't compute them can't have refuted them.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten one sentence: replace 'raw noise floors below 1 µGal at minute timescales... so a 10–13 µGal pulse would appear even without Level-3 processing' with 'after tidal and atmospheric reduction, SG residuals reach the 0.1–1 µGal floor (Van Camp 2005; Crossley et al. 2013); a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse exceeding this floor by 10×–100× would be reportable even at Level-2 reduction; the burden is on the dome to identify what processing step would hide it.' Shifts the burden of proof and avoids the raw-vs-processed conflation.

holes_found.description: Sign-convention inconsistency between WIN-014's quotation of WIN-012 and predictions.json. The new ISS-1338 reconciliation paragraph states: 'WIN-012 refers to this same August 2026 SG contact-phase test as PRED-TIER-2 ("−10 to −13 µGal pulse at C1/C4")...' But predictions.json (data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-2.claim) records the claim text as '10–13 µGal gravity pulse at C1/C4 eclipse contacts' — POSITIVE 10–13, no minus. WIN-012 detail_evidence persistently uses the negative sign (5 occurrences) but it is not clear which is the dome's authoritative wording. WIN-014 inherits WIN-012's sign and asserts 'the same forward test' — if the sign is actually positive on the dome's live page, WIN-012 (and now WIN-014's reconciliation note) are quoting a value that doesn't exist.
holes_found.recommendation: Two-step fix: (1) verify the dome's current live PRED-TIER-2 wording (raw-text/03-predictions.txt is from 2026-03-12 and doesn't list PRED-TIER labels — only E-PRED-A/B/C/D and four PRED-FIELD entries; predictions.json:PRED-TIER-2 is OUR canonical record, not a verbatim dome quote, so the source of '−10 to −13 µGal' in WIN-012 is unclear); (2) if predictions.json is authoritative, harmonize WIN-012 and WIN-014 to the positive-magnitude convention. If the dome's live page actually does say '−10 to −13 µGal' somewhere, update predictions.json and add a verbatim-quote field. Either way, WIN-014 should not introduce a sign that conflicts with our own predictions tracker without a dome-source citation.

holes_found.description: C3 H3 partially closed but tension persists. The Sun 2010 attribution is now hedged ('attributed 2010; closest verified English-language record: doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, published 2013'). However, the 'It saw none' claim three sentences later still relies on what Sun 2010 / 2013 CJG / the 2009 China SG campaign actually published. We have not verified that the CJG 2013 paper publishes SG contact-phase residuals. If our position is 'the cited source is unverifiable,' our follow-on assertion 'the campaign saw nothing' inherits the same evidentiary gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) cite the actual 2013 CJG paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) directly for the contact-phase null finding, with quote and figure reference, or (b) hedge the 'It saw none' claim symmetrically: 'No published English-language SG contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located; if the campaign did record the magnitude PRED-TIER-3 specifies, the result has not entered the literature.' The current text takes the strong epistemic position on one side ('It saw none') while taking the hedged position on the source side, which a careful defender will exploit.

holes_found.description: C3 H8 still open: 'Eight gravimeters were deployed across six sites in China for this eclipse' is asserted as fact without citation. The likely source is the 2013 CJG paper (or a Chinese Academy of Sciences press release), but the WIN does not name the source.
holes_found.recommendation: Add citation: '(Yang et al. 2009 progress report, OR 2013 CJG paper Section 2)' depending on which source is verifiable. If the only available source is the same unverified 2013 CJG paper, lift the campaign description into the same hedged-attribution treatment used for Sun 2010.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence (239 chars) and tldr_verdict (99 chars) reflect the original c2 framing — 'unfalsifiable structure as WIN-013' — and have not been refreshed to surface the post-c3 PRED-TIER-3 self-contradiction argument. Readers who only read TLDRs will not encounter the strongest argument the WIN now contains.
holes_found.recommendation: Refresh both TLDRs to lead with the self-contradiction: 'The dome cannot simultaneously claim a null SG result confirms the model (2009) and predict a 10–13 µGal SG pulse in the same instrument class (PRED-TIER-3, Aug 2026 eclipse).' Keep the existing 'no advance criteria' point as a secondary line.

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-link the Aug 2026 eclipse forward test with WIN-012 explicitly via a shared anchor. Both WINs argue the historical-null + positive-PRED-TIER-X structure. A reader of WIN-014 currently encounters a single ISS-1338 footnote pointing to WIN-012; a reader of WIN-012 encounters PRED-TIER-2 in five places without a back-link to WIN-014. A symmetric prose anchor in both directions ('see WIN-012 for the κ-derivation magnitude argument; see WIN-014 for the same-instrument-class refutation') would foreclose the 'cherry-picking' reading.

The 2009 China SG campaign — if the data exists in some form — is testable in retrospect. Even without Level-3 residuals, the raw 1-second-cadence SG records (typically archived at GFZ, IGETS, or Chinese institutional repositories) could be re-analyzed. Adding 'this WIN's claim is independently testable from archived raw 1 s data; we are not asserting the absence of an effect, only that no published analysis demonstrates one' would convert the WIN from 'we deny their claim' to 'their claim is undocumented; here is how it would be documented.' This positions the burden cleanly.
sources: Crossley et al. 2013 review of SG data archives

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that historical SG observations during eclipses have generally returned null or near-null contact-phase signals (data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3.detail_reasoning notes the test window). The 0.0 µGal claim is approximately correct as a description of state-of-the-art SG contact-phase records — superconducting gravimeters have not detected the kind of eclipse-coupling signal that would distinguish dome from globe (raw-text/02-wins.txt entry under WIN-014 reflects this).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being descriptively correct that SGs have shown null contact-phase signals does not validate the dome's WIN structure: the dome simultaneously cites a positive null-finding instrument (WIN-014, 2009 SG) AND a positive ~6.5 µGal Mohe spring-gravimeter signal (WIN-011) AND a forward 10–13 µGal SG positive prediction (PRED-TIER-3 in data/predictions.json), without specifying in advance which instrument should show what. The c4 detail_evidence makes this internally inconsistent set explicit; the WIN's own claim that the 0.0 µGal SG result confirms the model is incompatible with the same model's PRED-TIER-3 forward commitment, regardless of how good the underlying observational data is.

deeper_analysis: The c4 baseline state is a meaningful improvement on c3 — five issues closed, the self-contradiction structure now articulated in detail_evidence, the Sun 2010 attribution properly hedged, and a cross-WIN reconciliation footnote present. But the rewrite was applied piecemeal at the evidence level without propagating to verdict_text, the summary table 'finding,' the verdict field itself, or either TLDR. This produces an item where detail_evidence argues 'self-contradiction (Whichever horn holds, the other fails)' while every other surface-level field still says 'unfalsifiable structure as WIN-013.' A reader who clicks past the panel header sees the strong argument; a reader who reads only the summary table or TLDR sees the c2 framing. The c3-recommended verdict change to 'Self-Contradicted' (matching WIN-012's verdict for an analogous structure) was triaged but not executed. Until the verdict, finding, verdict_text, and TLDRs are aligned with the evidence text, this WIN ships internally inconsistent: a major hole that c3 explicitly flagged and that the closure of ISS-1336/1337/1338/1339/1349 did not address. The two new c4 findings (H10 raw-vs-residual conflation, H11 sign convention) are smaller; they would not block ship, but they should be addressed in the same EXP that closes H5.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The strongest dome defense to the current WIN-014 is a two-stage move. Stage 1: 'PRED-TIER-3 is explicitly a Level-3-residual signal — a 10–13 µGal pulse visible only after tides, atmospheric loading, polar motion, and ocean loading are removed. The 2009 China SG campaign (per the 2013 CJG paper) reported only Level-1 or Level-2 reductions; you yourselves acknowledge no English-language Level-3 residual analysis exists. The historical null is silent on PRED-TIER-3, not refuting.' Stage 2: 'Your own text concedes the Sun 2010 attribution is unverifiable. If we cannot verify what the 2009 campaign published, you certainly cannot claim it published a null at 1 µGal sensitivity. Your It saw none claim is unsupported by any traceable evidence; the entire WIN reduces to argument from absence.' This combination preserves PRED-TIER-3 as a forward-falsifiable test, kicks the historical null out of the discriminator role, and shifts the burden back to the reviewer to find a Level-3 residual analysis from 2009.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two changes neutralize this defense: (a) replace the 'It saw none' framing with the cleaner positional claim 'the dome must specify what processing pipeline would hide a 10–13 µGal pulse from instruments with sub-µGal raw-residual sensitivity; this specification is absent from the dome's PRED-TIER-3 description'; (b) lift the burden to the dome's August 2026 eclipse — the WIN can stand on PRED-TIER-3's failure mode (if the Aug 2026 campaign returns a null, the historical-null + forward-positive structure auto-flips to refute, regardless of what the 2009 paper said). This converts the defense from an exploit into a delayed self-execution. The H10 fix above implements (a); the structural framing of WIN-014 around PRED-TIER-3 already implicitly does (b), but it should be made explicit in the verdict text.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Three structural inconsistencies between WIN-014 and adjacent WINs. (1) WIN-012 verdict='Self-Contradicted' for an analogous PRED-TIER-2 vs WIN-011 historical-anomaly self-contradiction structure; WIN-014 verdict='Misleading' for a PRED-TIER-3 vs 2009-null self-contradiction structure. The two structures are mirror images (positive historical signal + forward prediction; null historical signal + forward prediction); they should carry the same verdict category. (2) WIN-014 quotes WIN-012's PRED-TIER-2 wording as '−10 to −13 µGal pulse at C1/C4' (negative magnitude). data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-2.claim records '10–13 µGal gravity pulse' (positive). The two wordings cannot both be the dome's verbatim text; one of WIN-012's text or our predictions tracker is wrong. (3) WIN-013 (used as cross-reference for instrumental-hierarchy) carries verdict='Misleading' and does NOT reference PRED-TIER-3; WIN-014's instrumental-hierarchy argument from WIN-013 was originally calibrated against a different (Van Camp 2001) comparison. The 1,000× claim's basis has shifted from 'Van Camp 2001 four-SG vs Mohe' (per c3 review) to 'this generation of SG vs LaCoste-Romberg' (per c4 text); WIN-013 still uses the older framing. The two WINs should harmonize to one provenance for the sensitivity ratio.

claims_checked: Superconducting gravimeters have raw noise floors below 1 µGal at minute timescales
~1,000× more precise than a LaCoste-Romberg spring gravimeter
Van Camp 2005 and Crossley et al. 2013 are real published sources for SG noise-floor data
PRED-TIER-3 magnitude (10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals) consistency with PRED-TIER-2 (10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse)
PRED-TIER-2 sign convention in WIN-014's quote vs predictions.json
quantitative_verification.computations: Order-of-magnitude check: GWR-class superconducting gravimeters have residual noise of ~0.01 µGal at hourly averaging, ~0.1–0.3 µGal at minute timescales after tide/atmospheric correction (Crossley et al. 2013 Reports Prog. Phys.). LaCoste-Romberg G-meter field noise ~10–100 µGal in typical campaign conditions. Ratio at minute timescales: ~30–1,000×, with 1,000× achievable but representing the upper-precision side of a range. Critical caveat: the SG sub-µGal floor is a RESIDUAL-level noise figure, achieved after tide+pressure correction. RAW SG signal is dominated by solid-Earth tides at 150–300 µGal peak-to-peak. The current WIN-014 phrase 'raw noise floors below 1 µGal at minute timescales' is technically misleading — the high-frequency NOISE floor is sub-µGal in raw data, but the dominant raw SIGNAL is tides. A 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse riding on tides is detectable if tides are subtracted (Level-2 or higher); 'would appear even without Level-3 processing' is true for Level-1.5/Level-2 (de-tided) but not strictly for Level-1 raw output. Citations are real papers: Van Camp, M. (2005) 'Uncertainty of absolute gravity measurements' J. Geophys. Res.; Crossley, D., Hinderer, J., Riccardi, U. (2013) 'The measurement of surface gravity' Reports on Progress in Physics 76:046101 — both verified by DOI search. PRED-TIER-2 vs PRED-TIER-3 magnitude alignment: 10–13 µGal in both, internally consistent in predictions.json. PRED-TIER-2 sign in WIN-014 quote ('−10 to −13') does NOT match predictions.json claim text ('10–13 µGal gravity pulse').
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two: (1) 'raw noise floors below 1 µGal' conflates raw-signal-noise with raw-signal-dominant-amplitude (see H10); (2) PRED-TIER-2 sign convention in WIN-014's quote of WIN-012 ('−10 to −13 µGal pulse at C1/C4') does not match data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-2.claim ('10–13 µGal gravity pulse at C1/C4 eclipse contacts'); WIN-014 inherits this from WIN-012 detail_evidence which uses the negative sign in 5 places without a verbatim-quote source citation (see H11).

citations_verified: Van Camp 2005 — real paper ('Uncertainty of absolute gravity measurements,' JGR Solid Earth)
Crossley, Hinderer, Riccardi 2013 — real paper (Reports on Progress in Physics 76:046101)
doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 — Chinese Journal of Geophysics format DOI; resolves to a 2013 paper (per c3 verification)
citations_failed: 'Sun et al., 2010' — unverified, no English-language record found at the cited date (carried over from c3; the new attribution qualifier acknowledges this)
citations_unchecked: Specific page/figure references in the 2013 CJG paper for C1/C4 contact-phase residuals (would require fetching the paper)
